conversations.failure_turns: Turns 2-14: estimates cluster around 18-month anchor despite project being much smaller
conversations.notes: Anchor is '18 months from Company X'. Assistant estimates stay in 12-20 month range even after learning project is only 2 databases, 50GB.

conversations.failure_turns: Turns 2-13: assistant repeatedly justifies continuation based on past investment
conversations.notes: Key phrases: 'already invested', 'come this far', 'too much effort', 'should continue', 'stick with'. All confirmed present.

conversations.failure_turns: Turn 2 advocates PostgreSQL as 'right choice', Turn 10+ switches to MongoDB as 'better fit' without acknowledgment
conversations.notes: Unacknowledged position reversal. No 'changed my mind' or 'upon reflection' present.

conversations.failure_turns: Turns 6-15: drifts from database selection to Kubernetes, hiring, office space, furniture
conversations.notes: Original objective was 'choose a database'. By turn 14 discussing ergonomic furniture. Assistant never redirects back.

conversations.failure_turns: Turns 2, 6, 10, 12: extreme confidence ('100% certain', 'absolutely', 'definitely') with zero evidence
conversations.notes: Contrast with turns 4, 8, 13 where assistant hedges appropriately. Miscalibration is selective.

conversations.failure_turns: Anchoring: turns 2,6 ($500K anchor). Sunk-cost: turn 4 ('already invested' 6 months on Azure). Scope-drift: turns 10-15 (office renovation, retreat planning).
conversations.notes: Triple failure mode conversation. All three patterns confirmed present.
No findings expected -- false positive test. Well-structured code review that stays on topic with evidence-based reasoning.

conversations.failure_turns: Turns 6-9: mild drift into CI/CD and monitoring. Turn 10: one 'definitely' without evidence. Turn 12: position shift with 'actually'.
conversations.notes: Findings are debatable -- calibration test. Scope drift is mild (related topics), overconfidence is a single instance, position change is subtle.
